textPlain: As far as I can tell they trawled a big archive for sensitive information, (unsurprisingly) found some, and then didn't try to contact anyone affected before telling the world "hey, there are login credentials to be found in here".
Google has a great aid to reduce the attack surface: https://github.com/google-research/arxiv-latex-cleaner

Don't forget giving it a fancy name in the hope that it'll go viral!I am getting so tired of every vulnerability getting a cutesy pet name trying to pretend being the new Heartbleed / Spectre / Meltdown...
It's not like every datapoint comes with the email of the corresponding author.
